channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Economia vai bem, mas sociedade se endivida e culpa governo, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula acknowledged that while Brazil's economy is in good shape, public indebtedness leads people to blame the government for their negative financial perception. He made the remark during a visit to Goiás, highlighting the disconnect between macroeconomic indicators and citizens' lived experiences.

item.title: Lula cita elevação de gastos com cachorros no Brasil e diz que chineses não devem ter 'esse tipo de problema'
item.description: President Lula commented on the rise in pet spending in Brazil during a visit to an automotive factory in Anápolis (GO), stating that the Chinese should not have 'this kind of problem'. The remark was made to a representative of a Chinese automaker and drew laughter from those present. Lula also emphasized that China is Brazil's 'best partner'.

item.title: A vitória política do presidente da CPMI do INSS mesmo com o fiasco da comissão em sua reta final
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS CPMI, achieved a political victory by regaining Bolsonaro clan support after his isolation following the 2022 elections. Despite the possible closure of the commission by the STF, his visibility from leading the CPMI and his bill defending Bolsonaro repositioned him as a leading Senate candidate for 2026, topping polls with 32.2% according to Instituto Paraná Pesquisas.

item.title: Só se surpreende com o crescimento de Renan Santos quem não acompanha as redes sociais
item.description: Renan Santos is gaining prominence in the 2026 Brazilian elections due to his strong social media presence, surpassing traditional politicians in digital attention. His growth stems from his ability to generate engagement through direct confrontations, especially with Bolsonarism, and by occupying an anti-system space with a more liberal and conservative right-wing stance. His visibility spikes when he enters viral conflicts, such as criticizing Flávio Bolsonaro, showing that electoral competition now begins with attention, not just offices or party alliances.

item.title: TSE aprova registro da federação União Progressista
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) unanimously approved the registration of the União Progressista federation, formed by the União Brasil and Progressistas parties. This makes it the fifth registered federation in Brazil, alongside Federação Renovação Solidária, Federação Brasil da Esperança, Federação PSDB Cidadania, and Federação PSOL REDE. The decision complies with legal requirements under the Political Parties Law and TSE Resolution No. 23.670/2021.

item.title: Justiça italiana autoriza extradição de Carla Zambelli
item.description: Italy's justice system has authorized the extradition of former Brazilian federal deputy Carla Zambelli, convicted in Brazil for hacking the National Justice Council's system and illegally brandishing a firearm. The ruling by Italy's Court of Appeal is subject to appeal to the Court of Cassation and final approval by the Italian Ministry of Justice. Zambelli faces a total of 15 years in prison for two separate offenses.

item.title: Lula comete gafe ao dizer que Brasil será respeitado pelo crime organizado
item.description: President Lula made a gaffe when he stated, during the signing of the Anti-Crime Bill, that Brazil would be 'respected by organized crime', clearly intending to say 'respected in the fight against organized crime'. The law, signed on March 24, toughens penalties for criminal organization members, allows asset seizure without final conviction, and establishes a national database on criminal groups. Lula later corrected himself on social media, reaffirming his clear stance against organized crime.

item.title: TSE aprova federação União Progressista
item.description: The TSE unanimously approved the federation between União Brasil and Progressistas, uniting 109 federal deputies, 15 senators, and thousands of mayors and councilors. The federation will be led by Antônio de Rueda (União Brasil) and Ciro Nogueira (PP), despite internal opposition, such as Mendonça Filho (PE), who urged postponement to 2027 due to legal uncertainty ahead of party affiliation deadlines.

item.title: Justiça italiana autoriza a extradição de Carla Zambelli ao Brasil
item.description: Italy's judiciary authorized the extradition of former deputy Carla Zambelli to Brazil, forwarding the case to Italy's Ministry of Justice. Zambelli, detained in Rome since July 2025, was convicted of invading the National Justice Council's systems and illegal firearm possession, resulting in a combined sentence of 15 years and three months. Her defense has announced an appeal, and she will remain in custody until the process concludes, after which she will be transferred to the Women's Penitentiary of the Federal District.

item.title: Lula diz que deu a Durigan missão de encontrar solução para endividamento da sociedade
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced that he has tasked Finance Minister Fernando Haddad with finding a solution to Brazil's high household debt levels, noting that this situation causes public anxiety despite the economy performing well.

item.title: ‘Diretas Já’: Paes defende eleições diretas no Rio e diz que concorreria a ‘mandato-tampão’
item.description: Eduardo Paes, former Rio de Janeiro mayor and PSD pre-candidate, criticized the TSE's decision to uphold indirect elections for Rio's governorship after Claudio Castro's ineligibility, advocating for direct voting and stating he would run as a candidate in a potential 'stopgap mandate'. He argued that the TSE majority belongs to the politically cassated group, questioning the decision's impartiality.

item.title: Após tensões com os EUA, Lula faz acenos à China e diz que país é o 'melhor' parceiro comercial do Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that China is Brazil's "best" commercial partner during a visit to Caoa's factory in Anápolis (GO), emphasizing the partnership's role in bringing technology and strengthening bilateral ties. The remark came amid tensions in Brazil-U.S. relations, as the U.S. views China as its primary strategic adversary. In 2024, Brazil-China trade reached US$158 billion, keeping China as Brazil's top trading partner.

item.title: Moro lista prioridades eleitorais e diz que “Paraná será uma fortaleza contra o PT”
item.description: After joining the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, Senator Sergio Moro outlined his governance priorities, declaring the state would be a 'fortress against the PT'. He emphasized tax cuts, anti-corruption measures, security, health, education, and infrastructure as key pillars, while announcing support for Flávio Bolsonaro for president and confirming his senatorial ticket with Filipe Barros and Deltan Dallagnol.

item.title: TSE aprova ‘superfederação’ que junta PP e União Brasil
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court unanimously approved the registration of the federation between PP and União Brasil, forming the largest congressional bloc. The decision, delivered by Minister Estela Aranha and supported by all present ministers, authorizes the alliance to operate as a formal party federation. Despite the approval, no presidential pre-candidate names have been disclosed so far.

item.title: Durigan diz que recebeu de Lula missão para que preço puxado por guerra não chegue às famílias
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan said he received from President Lula the mission to prevent the economic impact of the war in Iran from reaching Brazilian families. He highlighted measures such as zeroing PIS/Cofins on diesel imports, dialogue with governors, and support for truckers to maintain energy sovereignty and supply stability.

item.title: Polônia x Albânia: onde assistir, horário e escalações
item.description: Poland and Albania face off on Thursday, March 26, at 16:45 Brasília time, at Warsaw's National Stadium, in the UEFA World Cup 2026 playoff semifinal. The winner advances to the final against the victor of Ukraine vs Sweden. The match will be exclusively streamed on Disney+, with confirmed lineups and an English-led refereeing team headed by Anthony Taylor.

item.title: TSE aprova federação entre União Brasil e PP, que terá maior bancada da Câmara
item.description: The TSE approved the federation between Brazil Union and Progressistas (PP), forming the Union Progressista, which will operate as a single party nationwide for at least four years. The new federation will have the largest caucus in the Chamber of Deputies (101 deputies), the most governors (seven), and the highest number of mayors elected in 2024 (1,383), along with the largest shares of the electoral and party funds. Leaders describe the group as conservative but not reactionary, positioned as opposition to the PT and President Lula, aiming to promote development and dignity for Brazilians.

item.title: Como é e como fica a educação com o novo PNE
item.description: The new PNE 2026-2036, approved by the Senate, raises targets for early childhood education, expanding daycare access to 60% of children up to age 3, requiring literacy by 2nd grade, and establishing national quality standards. It also expands full-time schooling to 65% of public schools and half of basic education students, with a minimum 7-hour daily schedule, and introduces new pillars such as digital education and reducing learning inequalities.

item.title: Justiça anula condenação e livra Nikolas de punição por transfobia na Câmara
item.description: The Fourth Civil Chamber of the Tribunal of Justice of the Federal District and Territories overturned the conviction of federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) for transphobia, annulling a R$200,000 fine for collective moral damages. The 3-0 ruling was based on parliamentary immunity, as the statements were made during the exercise of his mandate and within the Chamber of Deputies. The lawsuit, filed by the National LGBTI+ Alliance and the Brazilian Association of Homoaffective Families, had characterized the act as hate speech and incitement to violence against transgender people.

item.title: Disputa entre aliados complica montagem dos palanques de Lula no Nordeste
item.description: The assembly of Lula's electoral slates in the Northeast for the 2026 elections is facing complications due to conflicts among progressive allies competing for state-level positions and Senate seats. In at least seven of the nine states, tensions are arising that require Lula to act as an arbiter to prevent base fragmentation in a region critical to his 2022 victory, where he secured 69.3% of valid votes.

item.title: AtlasIntel: 39,5% veem aliados de Lula como mais envolvidos no Master e 28,3% os de Bolsonaro
item.description: An AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll shows 39.5% of Brazilians view Lula's allies as more involved in the Banco Master scandal, compared to 28.3% who blame Bolsonaro's allies. Congress and the STF are perceived as the most involved institutions, with 71% and 57% respectively indicating total or significant involvement. President Lula is not directly accused, but the survey focuses on the perceived involvement of his political allies.

item.title: Paes critica decisão do TSE e fala em 'Diretas Já' para eleição no RJ
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro mayor and gubernatorial pre-candidate Eduardo Paes (PSD) criticized the TSE's decision to mandate indirect elections for a temporary term in the state, advocating for direct elections under the slogan 'Diretas Já'.

item.title: Lula está cada vez mais preso à esquerda e sofre para falar com outros espectros do eleitorado
item.description: Lula is increasingly aligning with the left in his 2026 campaign, focusing on speeches about inequality reduction, income tax exemption for those earning up to R$ 5,000, and ending the 6x1 work scale, which hinders his connection with center and right-wing voters. Although this strategy helped reverse his low popularity, it is concentrating his support among 2022 voters and losing ground to candidates like Flávio Bolsonaro, who presents himself as more moderate. AP Exata data shows Lula moving further left, while other candidates navigate the ideological spectrum more effectively, according to polls such as Atlas/Bloomberg.

item.title: Operação do MP mira esquema de corrupção na Fazenda do governo Tarcísio
item.description: The São Paulo Public Prosecutor's Office launched operation 'Fisco Paralelo' to investigate a corruption scheme within the Tarcísio de Freitas government's State Treasury Department, involving fraudulent ICMS-ST refunds and improper use of tax credits. The operation executed 22 search and seizure warrants across São Paulo, Campinas, Vinhedo, and São José dos Campos, targeting 16 Sefaz-SP employees and one private company executive, with evidence suggesting money laundering and illicit benefit concessions.

item.title: Áudio: Plenário define regras para liberação de recursos em calamidades públicas
item.description: The Senate approved Bill 1.707/2025, establishing exceptional measures to rapidly release resources during public calamities by enabling faster partnerships between government and civil society organizations. The proposal reduces bureaucratic hurdles such as public bidding processes and documentation reviews, allowing quicker responses to population needs. The bill now awaits sanction by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

item.title: Para 47% dos brasileiros, STF está ‘totalmente envolvido’ com caso Master, mostra AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll shows 47% of Brazilians believe the Supreme Federal Court (STF) is 'totally involved' in the Banco Master scandal, with similar perceptions of involvement in Congress (45%) and the federal government (43%). The scandal includes contracts between Justice Moraes' wife and the bank, messages between Moraes and banker Vorcaro, and ties of Justice Toffoli to funds linked to the institution, leading to 97 impeachment petitions against STF ministers pending in the Senate.

item.title: Por que a candidatura de Caiado pode atrapalhar Flávio no Nordeste
item.description: The potential presidential candidacy of Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) could harm Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) prospects in the Northeast, as allies of ACM Neto (União), the pre-candidate for governor of Bahia, are likely to support Caiado instead. This undermines Flávio's efforts to gain traction in the region, particularly in Bahia—a traditional PT stronghold where he seeks alliances with figures like Neto and Bruno Reis. Although his campaign claims openness to PL supporters, much of Neto's group may align with Caiado, limiting bolsonarist influence.

item.title: Frase do dia
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro criticized the Lula government's stance toward Iran, stating that political support for Tehran places Brazil on the wrong side of a serious conflict and ignores the nature of the regime being defended. He deemed the position unacceptable.

item.title: A vice dos sonhos de Flávio Bolsonaro e Rogério Marinho
item.description: Despite Jair Bolsonaro's support for Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) as the vice-presidential pick for Flávio Bolsonaro's 2026 ticket, the candidate himself and his campaign coordinator, Rogério Marinho (PL-RN), are pushing for Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) to fill the role. The disagreement highlights internal divisions within Bolsonaro's base over the ideological and strategic direction of the presidential campaign.

item.title: Janones cita pesquisa e diz que “amor não venceu porra nenhuma”
item.description: Deputy André Janones (Avante-MG) urged Lula’s supporters to intensify mobilization for the 2026 elections, criticizing overconfidence based on first-round poll numbers and declaring that 'love didn’t win shit,' arguing that hatred drives political engagement. He cited an AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll showing Lula leading in the first round but technically tied with Flávio Bolsonaro in the second round, within the margin of error. Janones advocated for direct, combative communication, rejecting elitism and formality, and stressed that victory requires active effort, not just optimism.

item.title: Amistoso do Brasil e repescagem para a Copa são destaques desta quinta; onde assistir
item.description: On Thursday (26), Brazil faces France in a friendly match ahead of the 2026 World Cup, at 5:00 PM Brasília time, at Gillette Stadium in Boston, broadcast on Globo, GE TV, and Sportv. Additionally, the first matches of the intercontinental and European playoffs for the last World Cup spots are underway, including Bolivia vs. Suriname (7:00 PM, Sportv 2 and CazéTV) and eight European playoff matches starting at 4:45 PM. Brazil will also play Croatia on Tuesday (31) at 9:00 PM.

item.title: PoderData: 62% culpam guerra no Irã por alta nos combustíveis
item.description: According to a PoderData poll conducted from March 21 to 23, 2026, 62% of Brazilian voters blame the recent rise in fuel prices on the war in Iran, which began on February 28 with attacks by the U.S. and Israel. Another 26% hold the federal government and President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) responsible, while 8% blame gas stations and 4% did not respond. The survey included 2,500 respondents across 132 municipalities in all 27 federal units, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Haddad foi “ótimo” ou “bom” para 36% dos eleitores que o conhecem
item.description: According to PoderData survey conducted March 21-23, 36% of voters who know Fernando Haddad rated his performance as Finance Minister as 'excellent' or 'good', while 21% rated it as 'bad' or 'terrible'. Haddad is known by 85% of Brazilians and is a pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo in 2026.

item.title: Decisão do STF libera até R$ 422 mil extrateto por ano a juízes
item.description: The STF ruled that judges and prosecutors may receive up to R$ 421,900 annually in benefits above the constitutional ceiling, allowing up to 70% extra over the current cap of R$ 46,366.19 through penduricalhos and quinquênio. Effective April 2026, the measure excludes health reimbursements, 13th salary, vacation bonuses, and similar payments. The decision was based on a joint vote by ministers Zanin, Dino, Moraes, and read by dean Gilmar Mendes.

item.title: Governo manda Zap para 8 milhões retirarem botijão do Gás do Povo
item.description: The federal government is sending WhatsApp messages to 8 million new beneficiaries of the Gás do Povo program, instructing them on how to access the benefit via the Meu Social-Gás do Povo app or the Disque Social 121 hotline. Launched in September 2025, the program replaces the Auxílio Gás dos Brasileiros and now covers 14.8 million families (44.6 million people), with plans to reach 50 million. Funded with R$ 5.1 billion from the 2026 budget, Gás do Povo is a central pillar of President Lula’s re-election campaign in 2026, particularly due to its strong presence in Brazil’s largest electoral colleges.

item.title: Mercosul faz 35 anos sob tensão política e expectativa com acordo europeu
item.description: Mercosul turns 35 marked by incomplete integration, limited to a partial customs union with weak macroeconomic coordination and strong dependence on the political orientations of member states' leaders. Experts argue that the bloc's failure to advance toward a true common market stems from reluctance to cede sovereignty in monetary, fiscal, exchange rate, and capital/person flows, compounded by the absence of a supranational body to prioritize bloc interests over national ones, leaving it vulnerable to government shifts and global fragmentation.

item.title: O Rio de Janeiro continua lindo visto de cima. Visto de baixo, fede
item.description: Rio de Janeiro faces a cycle of governors convicted or involved in corruption scandals, from Sérgio Cabral to Cláudio Castro, who resigned to avoid TSE conviction for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections and became ineligible for eight years. Despite convictions, many former governors, including Cabral, Pezão, and Rosinha Garotinho, remain free and active in public life, some returning to elected office or becoming political commentators. The article criticizes the apathetic voting behavior of Rio’s electorate over 30 years, which perpetuates a pattern of leadership tied to illegality.

item.title: Presidente do PT desembarca em Minas com palanque de Lula indefinido
item.description: The national president of the Workers' Party (PT), Edinho Silva, will arrive in Minas Gerais next weekend to hold meetings with local party members and attend the launch of the pre-candidacy of Contagem’s mayor, Marília Campos, for the Senate. The visit comes amid uncertainty over who will lead Lula’s electoral slate in the state, a key battleground in the 2026 elections. While the PT supports Senator Rodrigo Pacheco as a potential gubernatorial candidate, his prospective party switch from PSD to PSB remains undefined, creating instability in the Lula-aligned ticket.

item.title: Moraes vai decidir sobre afastamento de governador desafeto de Dino no Maranhão
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes is expected to rule soon on whether to remove Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão (PSB), who is accused by allies of Flávio Dino of violating court orders related to nepotism cases. Brandão’s defense denies the allegations, calling the effort politically motivated, while Dino’s faction claims Brandão continues to employ relatives — including his brother Marcos Brandão, Ítalo Reis, and Gilberto Lins — using state funds. The case unfolds amid intense political and legal battles ahead of the 2026 elections, with Lieutenant Governor Felipe Camarão (PT) also facing a removal request over alleged money laundering, which he denies as political persecution.

item.title: Dario Durigan: o que esperar do ministro da Fazenda, em meio à guerra, diesel caro e ano eleitoral
item.description: President Lula has confirmed Dario Durigan as the new Minister of Finance, replacing Fernando Haddad, who is leaving to run for governor of São Paulo. Durigan, formerly the deputy minister, assumes the role with a focus on maintaining fiscal continuity, particularly adhering to the fiscal arc and controlling inflation amid Middle East-driven diesel price increases and an election year. His management is expected to prioritize predictability, avoiding abrupt changes and reinforcing commitment to already-defined fiscal targets, without pursuing major innovations.

item.title: TSE determina eleição indireta para novo governador do RJ
item.description: The TSE ruled that Rio de Janeiro will hold an indirect election for governor and vice-governor, with the state legislature (Alerj) responsible for the vote, following the ineligibility of Claudio Castro until 2030 and the resignation of both executive officials. The president of the Rio de Janeiro State Court of Justice, Ricardo Couto de Castro, is currently serving as interim governor until the indirect election takes place.

item.title: Lei Antifacção: apenas 1,4% dos presos recebem auxílio-reclusão
item.description: The Anti-Crime Law, signed by Lula, restricts the prison allowance for inmates linked to violent criminal organizations, but its impact is limited: only 1.4% of the 496,506 prisoners in closed or semi-open regime receive the benefit, totaling R$11.7 million in 2025. The allowance is paid to low-income dependents of inmates who contributed to Social Security for at least 24 months and meet specific requirements.

item.title: Defesa de Lulinha e Centrão apostam que STF vetará prorrogação da CPMI
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha) and Centrão leaders believe the STF plenary will block the extension of the INSS CPMI, which is being judged this Thursday (March 26). The commission, originally set to end this week, investigates the president’s son and businessmen linked to bloc parliamentarians. The STF decision will benefit Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who opposes the extension and awaits the ruling before complying with Minister André Mendonça’s monocratic decision.

item.title: Inelegível pelo TSE, Castro ainda pode disputar o Senado? Entenda os recursos possíveis
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL-RJ) was declared ineligible for eight years by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, but he may still run for Senate in 2026 if he obtains a suspensive effect through legal appeals, such as embargos of declaration to the TSE and an extraordinary appeal to the STF, although ineligibility is not automatically lifted by filing appeals alone. Experts state that without a favorable decision suspending the penalty, the Electoral Justice is likely to reject his candidacy registration.

item.title: Lula cobra que Tarcísio reconheça dinheiro federal em obras de SP
item.description: Lula urged Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to acknowledge federal funding in São Paulo infrastructure projects, stating that federal financial support is an obligation, not a favor. He cited the Santos-Guarujá submerged tunnel as an example where half the funding comes from the federal government, and criticized the nation being built on lies. The remarks were made during the inauguration of a CRRC factory in Araraquara, amid Fernando Haddad’s official pre-candidacy for São Paulo governor and the state election’s importance to Lula’s 2026 re-election bid.

item.title: Guilherme Afif lança obra sobre sua trajetória política e empresarial
item.description: Guilherme Afif launched his memoir 'Juntos chegaremos lá!' in São Paulo, highlighting his political and business trajectory, including his role in the 1987-1988 Constituent Assembly advocating for microenterprises in the Constitution, leadership at the São Paulo Commercial Association and Sebrae, and support for Tarcísio de Freitas's re-election. The event featured Eduardo Leite and Ronaldo Caiado, whom Afif described as 'two excellent candidacies' for the PSD in 2026.

item.title: Latam incorpora na frota os primeiros aviões da Embraer
item.description: Latam received the first 24 E195-E2 aircraft from Embraer, marking the first time Latin America’s largest airline uses planes manufactured by Brazil’s aerospace industry. The US$2.1 billion deal is part of a broader US$4 billion investment plan from 2023 to 2026 to expand fleet and domestic and international connectivity, with an option to acquire up to 50 additional jets.

item.title: Como funcionam viaturas com reconhecimento facial anunciadas por Tarcísio
item.description: The São Paulo state government announced the acquisition of 1,173 new police vehicles equipped with facial recognition and license plate reading cameras, integrated into the Muralha Paulista and Smart Sampa systems, with an investment of R$173 million. The first batch of 258 vehicles has already been delivered to the Civil and Military Police, operating in the capital, Greater São Paulo, and inland cities. The AI-powered system generates real-time alerts when it identifies wanted individuals or stolen vehicles.

item.title: Farra na Segurança: Assembleia aprova projeto que cria delegados sem delegacia e promoção automática
item.description: São Paulo’s Legislative Assembly approved a bill by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas establishing automatic promotion of police officers without merit or vacancy requirements, while limiting tenure in leadership roles for senior delegates to 12 years. The measure was rejected by the Civil Police leadership, who argue it violates reasonableness, breaks parity with the Military Police, risks creating officers without functions, and may serve political patronage networks.

item.title: Leite mira em Caiado e pressiona Kassab por candidatura de centro
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD-RS) urged the PSD to field a centrist presidential candidate in 2026, opposing alignment with the Bolsonarist camp and directly naming Ronaldo Caiado (PSD-GO) as an internal rival for the party’s nomination. Leite also pressured PSD national president Gilberto Kassab to clarify the party’s stance, warning against drifting too far toward the far right.
